✦
A Lifelong Fan

Disney & Marvel have been part of my story long before my career.

I'm a genuine fan — genuine enough that, while my classmates posed in caps and gowns, I wore a handmade Iron Man suit for my high school graduation photo. That wasn't a one-off joke; it was a small declaration of what these stories mean to me.

To me, the worlds Disney and Marvel create have never been just entertainment. They're brands I've grown up with and live alongside every day — the stories that shaped how I think about imagination, craft, and wonder. Helping bring those experiences to audiences in Korea wouldn't simply be a job; it would be a continuation of a story I've been living for years.

From fan in costume → marketer ready to build the magic
About

Profile

Experience Six years in global marketing operations

For the past six years at ZEISS Korea, I have coordinated marketing activities across digital and offline channels while working with APAC teams, global stakeholders, external agencies, and local business units. My role has included managing marketing assets, localizing global content for Korean audiences, coordinating campaign timelines, and ensuring brand consistency across multiple touchpoints.

Motivation Why Disney

Disney was one of the few global brands I paid attention to long before I started my career in marketing. As I gained experience in a global organization, I became increasingly interested in how Disney consistently delivers high-quality content and brand experiences across different markets, languages, and platforms. I would value the opportunity to support a brand that I have respected both as a consumer and as a marketer.

Core skills & tools Marketing Operations Content Localization Campaign Coordination Digital Asset Management Stakeholder Management Cross-functional Collaboration Project Coordination Bilingual Communication EN/KR Adobe Marketo CRM AEM Meta Google Kakao Naver
Selected Work

Case Studies

Five operational cases spanning localization, multi-channel asset management, regional coordination, audience content, and marketing automation.

01

Localizing Global Content for Korean Audiences

Content LocalizationGlobal-to-Local Execution Brand ConsistencyAEMStakeholder Communication
Challenge

Global marketing campaigns often required adaptation for Korean audiences while maintaining the original brand message and global standards.

Actions
  • Localized global campaign and product content for the Korean market.
  • Managed website, landing page, and campaign content using Adobe Experience Manager (AEM).
  • Adapted messaging, visuals, and copy to improve local relevance while preserving brand consistency.
  • Coordinated with global stakeholders to ensure alignment with campaign objectives and brand guidelines.
Impact
  • Successfully delivered localized content across websites, landing pages, newsletters, and campaign materials.
  • Strengthened the balance between global consistency and local relevance.
  • Developed a strong understanding of content localization workflows and stakeholder communication.

Localized landing page & campaign content managed in Adobe Experience Manager (KR).

02

Managing Marketing Assets Across Multiple Channels

Digital Asset ManagementCampaign Coordination Multi-channel MarketingVendor ManagementBrand Guideline
Challenge

Marketing campaigns required the coordination of multiple content formats, channels, and stakeholders while ensuring timely delivery and consistent messaging.

Actions
  • Managed campaign assets across websites, digital advertising, social media, webinars, exhibitions, and customer events.
  • Created and coordinated brochures, invitations, videos, event materials, digital banners, and landing pages.
  • Worked closely with designers, vendors, sales teams, and product managers to deliver assets on schedule.
  • Maintained content quality and brand consistency across all customer touchpoints.
Impact
  • Delivered integrated marketing campaigns across online and offline channels.
  • Improved operational efficiency through structured asset management and stakeholder coordination.
  • Gained hands-on experience managing multiple deadlines and deliverables simultaneously.

Integrated assets across exhibitions, customer events, print and digital touchpoints.

03

Supporting Regional Marketing Operations

Regional MarketingCross-functional Collaboration Timeline ManagementCampaign OperationsStakeholder Management
Challenge

Marketing activities involved collaboration between local teams, global stakeholders, external vendors, and business units, requiring clear communication and coordination.

Actions
  • Acted as a liaison between local business teams and global marketing stakeholders.
  • Coordinated campaign requests, approvals, and implementation schedules.
  • Supported product launches, workshops, webinars, exhibitions, and customer engagement programs.
  • Managed multiple projects concurrently while ensuring timely communication among stakeholders.
Impact
  • Contributed to the successful execution of marketing programs across multiple business segments.
  • Strengthened cross-functional communication and project management capabilities.
  • Developed a strong understanding of operating within a global marketing organization.

Regional campaign execution across social, search and landing-page flows.

04

Creating and Managing Audience-Focused Content

Audience EngagementOwned Channel Social ContentShort-form VideoContent Planning
Challenge

Different audiences and communication channels required tailored content formats while maintaining a consistent brand experience.

Actions
  • Managed customer communications through owned channels including KakaoTalk and email marketing.
  • Created graphics, videos, social content, and written communications for specific audience groups.
  • Adapted content formats based on channel characteristics and audience behavior.
  • Planned and executed short-form content designed to improve engagement and reach.
Impact
  • Improved understanding of audience engagement and content performance.
  • Strengthened content planning and video production capabilities.
  • Developed practical experience in creating content that resonates with specific communities and interests.

Personal Project — O-Daeri Lunchbox Channel

2M+ views · 6,900+ followers

Created and operated a short-form social media channel focused on lunchbox reviews. Managed content planning, filming, editing, publishing, and community engagement independently — achieving over 2 million cumulative views and 6,900+ followers through audience-focused storytelling and consistent content production.

View Instagram Project →

Owned-channel messaging (KakaoTalk) and the @dosirak_bboy short-form channel (2M+ views).

05

Marketing Operations & Customer Data Management

Marketing AutomationCRM Customer DataSegmentationCampaign Workflow
Challenge

Marketing campaigns generated large volumes of customer interactions that required structured management and effective follow-up.

Actions
  • Managed customer data and campaign operations using Adobe Marketo and CRM platforms.
  • Supported lead qualification, segmentation, and campaign workflows.
  • Maintained opt-in data and audience records to improve communication accuracy.
  • Collaborated with sales teams to support follow-up activities and customer engagement programs.
Impact
  • Improved campaign execution through structured data and workflow management.
  • Enhanced collaboration between marketing and sales teams.
  • Developed strong operational capabilities in marketing automation and customer lifecycle management.
